headline: Marine Le Pen to find out whether she can run for president
description: A court will rule on Tuesday in Marine Le Pen's appeal trial over alleged misappropriation of European Union funds. Its decision will determine whether she can run in the 2027 presidential election.

headline: Explosions rock Damascus during Macron's visit

As US disengages from NATO, Europe's militaries ramp up scattered and unsynchronized
description: In Ankara on July 7 and 8, European leaders from NATO want to show that they can cope with reduced US involvement and boost their defense capabilities to respond to any attack. While efforts are now underway, Europe is still struggling to coordinate them.

headline: Netanyahu government's legal coup challenges Israel's justice system: 'The final nail in the coffin of democracy'
description: By declaring he would not comply with a Supreme Court ruling, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is directly confronting the country's highest legal authority, plunging Israel into a constitutional crisis.

headline: One hundred early works by Claude Monet reunited at Le Havre's André Malraux Museum
description: The MuMa exhibition, showcasing drawings, caricatures and paintings, places the painter in his family and regional context.

headline: France faces new heatwave with temperatures reaching 40°C
description: Heat swept across much of the country on Monday, with temperatures reaching up to 38°C and even 40°C in the southwest. The third major heatwave of the year is expected to be both intense and prolonged, likely lasting around 12 days.

Somaliland's high-risk quest for recognition
description: The self-proclaimed republic aspires to accede to the international stage. It finds itself at the center of a game of alliances, caught between Israeli, American and Emirati ambitions and threats from Yemen and neighboring Somalia.

headline: Resigned Le Pen puts on brave face before crucial court decision
description: In her final public appearance before the ruling on Tuesday that will determine whether she can run for president, Marine Le Pen focused on highlighting her partnership with Jordan Bardella.

headline: 'What comes next will be just as grueling': Villagers remain anxious after wildfire in southern France
description: A wildfire fanned by fierce winds amid extreme heat consumed 540 hectares of scrubland in the Gard department within a matter of hours.
